content.full_text:
February 13, 1992

Carla Wardlo
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Wardlo:

This letter is to confirm the information you provided to me over the phone this afternoon. That information is as follows:

(1) I am not an "intermediary" pursuant to California Government Code § 84302 if checks made payable to a candidate's committee are mailed to my P.O. Box and then I forward those checks on to the committee's treasurer within three days of receipt.

(2) I am not regulated by California Government Code § 84305 If I pay for, prepare, and mail no more than 200 invitations to a fundraiser for a candidate. In other words, there is no requirements (under this law) as to what information is (or is not) included on the invitations, reply cards, or envelopes, because this section applies only when "200 or more identical, or nearly identical, pieces of mail have been sent within a calendar month."

If this is not an accurate account of our conversation, please let me know. In addition, I would appreciate copies of any advisory letters or regulations supporting these positions. Thank You.

Very truly yours,

Bill Baber
